overview: Slivers is a Pauper aggro deck that leverages multiple lords to buff a swarm of creatures, granting shared abilities like evasion, mana production, and combat tricks. It utilizes a high number of draw spells for consistency and can run two to four colors depending on the desired keywords. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly while using card advantage to navigate sweepers and maintain pressure.
win_conditions: Attacking with a large board of buffed Slivers
Utilizing shared abilities like flying, poison, and combat tricks to close games
Outdrawing opponents to rebuild the board after sweepers

keep_criteria: Hands with early Slivers and lords to establish pressure
Hands containing draw spells to maintain consistency
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing Plains/Forest for early 1-drops), at least one early Sliver (Predatory, Plated, or Muscle), and a clear path to cast it by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands completely lacking early creatures or lords
Hands that are severely color-screwed despite fixing options
Mulligan to 5 if you have 0-1 lands, multiple uncastable Slivers, or no early threats.

examples.reason: Provides early board presence, card draw, and mana fixing to start attacking quickly, aligning with the deck's low mulligan rate.
Lacks early creatures or lords to apply pressure, relying too heavily on late-game or sideboard cards despite draw potential.

early_game: Play Slivers and lords quickly to establish board presence
Use draw spells early to empty hand and refill for consistency
Turn 1: Play a basic land matching your earliest Sliver's mana cost (Plains for Predatory/Plated, Forest for Muscle).
If you have a 1-drop, cast it.
Turn 2: Play another land, then cast a second Sliver or a 2-drop utility Sliver (e.g., Sliver Hive/Queen).
Sequence lands to ensure you can hit 2 and 3 mana reliably
fetch/shocks are ideal if your mana base supports them.
mid_game: Expand the swarm and utilize shared abilities like evasion and combat tricks
Hold extra lords in hand to navigate sweepers
Force favorable trades using abilities like Hunter Sliver's provoke
always maintain 1-2 mana open to react to sweepers or cast multiple threats.
Prioritize casting Sliver Queen/Hive/Overlord to generate card advantage and replace lost threats.
Avoid hoarding mana if you can safely commit to a board that trades favorably.
Commit threats when you have protection (e.g., lifelink/first strike trading into removal) or when the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back if you suspect a board wipe or spot removal
late_game: Leverage card advantage to rebuild the board after sweepers
Continue attacking with a wide, buffed swarm to close out games
Use sideboard cards like Quick Sliver to cast threats on opponent's turn
A board of 5+ Slivers with trample, haste, or lifelink, often fueled by Sliver Queen/Overlord card advantage.
Win via combat if you can break through blockers, or via a sudden lethal turn when the opponent is low.
Go all-in when you have enough power to trade favorably into blockers, when the opponent has no sweepers in hand, or when you can close the game in 1-2 turns.
Also commit when you've resolved a Sliver engine and can replace losses immediately.

key_cards.name: Winged Sliver
key_cards.notes: Grants flying to the swarm and helps deal with flying creatures.

key_cards.name: Winding Way
key_cards.notes: Provides consistency to empty hand and draw more cards for pressure.

key_cards.name: Lead the Stampede
key_cards.notes: Draws cards based on creature count, fueling the swarm strategy.

key_cards.name: Gemhide Sliver
key_cards.notes: Fixes colors and speeds up casting multiple spells in a single turn.

key_cards.name: Masked Vandal
key_cards.notes: Exiles indestructible lands and counts as a Sliver, strong vs Affinity.

key_cards.name: Weather the Storm
key_cards.notes: Counters spells to survive against Burn decks.

key_cards.name: Scattershot Archer
key_cards.notes: Provides reach to combat Faeries and other flying threats.

key_cards.name: Quick Sliver
key_cards.notes: Allows casting threats on the opponent's turn to apply pressure.

sequencing_tips: Play lords early to maximize swarm buffs
Cast draw spells to maintain hand size and consistency
Hold lords in hand to dodge sweepers like Electrickery
Use Quick Sliver on the opponent's turn to play threats for free
Common mistake: Overextending into sweepers without a backup plan.
Remember that Slivers share abilities, so losing one creature doesn't strip your board of keywords.
Unique interaction: Sliver Hive/Queen/Overlord create a recursive value loop
Also, track opponent's life total closely, as Slivers often win by trading up and going face rather than grinding for a single massive threat.

matchups.advice: Be as fast as possible to win before they deal lethal damage. Side in Weather the Storm to protect your board.
Exploit their slow board development. Side in Masked Vandal to exile indestructible lands and maintain lord count.
Play aggressively fast to force bad trades. Side in Scattershot Archer for reach and Quick Sliver to cast threats on their turn.
A difficult matchup. Try to damage the opponent without losing creatures to enable their dungeon. Sideboard offers little help.
A mirror where abilities share with the opponent. Predatory Sliver is the key advantage. Win by drawing more cards to maintain board presence.

Cast multiple cheap threats in response to counterspells to force them to spend interaction. Use uncounterable effects if available, or simply out-value them by committing more threats than they can counter.
Apply early pressure to finish them before they deploy their finishers. Use spot removal on key ramp targets and prioritize trading Slivers efficiently to stall their development.

Stabilize with early lifelink/first strike Slivers (Predatory/Plated) to trade into their attackers. Don't overcommit to a board that can be wiped; focus on reaching a critical mass of Slivers that can turn the tables with trample or haste.

Pressure relentlessly with multiple threats per turn to prevent them from stabilizing. Use Sliver Queen/Hive/Overlord to out-grind their card advantage. Don't let them sweep your board without threatening lethal or having a backup plan.

It's a race to card advantage and late-game power. Prioritize Sliver Queen/Overlord to generate value. Win by out-grinding, having better late-game threats, and forcing them to trade inefficiently. Sideboard in more value engines and hate for their specific Slivers.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Toxic Deluge, etc.), spot removal (Path to Exile, Lightning Strike), and combo finishers.
Note when opponents hold up mana past turn 3, as they likely have interaction.
Track their life total to gauge if you should race or grind.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Sliver, the tribe with the most lords in Pauper is also a strong aggro? ... A differential in our deck is the amount of draw spells: there are 8 ... Our stance is like any aggro deck: make several strong creatures and attack.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Our stance is like any aggro deck: make several strong creatures and attack.
we can easily make our creatures 3/3, 4/4 or even more.
draw more cards to continue pressuring the opponents.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: with the number of lords, we almost always manage to escape the sweepers
cast our threats at their turn
empty the hand and draw more cards to continue pressuring the opponents

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: escape the sweepers like Electrickery , Fiery Cannonade , Breath Weapon and now also Arms of Hadar
removals are only 4 Galvanic Blast and Makeshift Munitions
everything will depend on Spellstutter Sprite
